speaker: AGAMEMNON

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Agamemnon,

speaker: ULYSSES

text: What heart receives from hence a conquering part

speaker: NESTOR
text: To steel a strong opinion to themselves?—

speaker: NESTOR
text: Which entertained, limbs are his instruments,

speaker: NESTOR
text: In no less working than are swords and bows

speaker: NESTOR
text: Directive by the limbs.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Give pardon to my speech: therefore ’tis meet

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Achilles meet not Hector. Let us like merchants

speaker: ULYSSES
text: First show foul wares and think perchance they’ll sell;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: If not, the luster of the better shall exceed

speaker: ULYSSES
text: By showing the worse first. Do not consent

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That ever Hector and Achilles meet,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: For both our honor and our shame in this

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Are dogged with two strange followers.

speaker: NESTOR
text: I see them not with my old eyes. What are they?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: What glory our Achilles shares from Hector,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Were he not proud, we all should share with him;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: But he already is too insolent,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And it were better parch in Afric sun

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Than in the pride and salt scorn of his eyes

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Should he scape Hector fair. If he were foiled,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Why then we do our main opinion crush

speaker: ULYSSES
text: In taint of our best man. No, make a lott’ry,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: And, by device, let blockish Ajax draw

speaker: ULYSSES
text: The sort to fight with Hector. Among ourselves

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Give him allowance for the better man,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: For that will physic the great Myrmidon,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Who broils in loud applause, and make him fall

speaker: ULYSSES
text: His crest that prouder than blue Iris bends.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: If the dull brainless Ajax come safe off,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: We’ll dress him up in voices; if he fail,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Yet go we under our opinion still

speaker: ULYSSES
text: That we have better men. But, hit or miss,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Our project’s life this shape of sense assumes:

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Ajax employed plucks down Achilles’ plumes.

speaker: NESTOR
text: Now, Ulysses, I begin to relish thy advice,

speaker: NESTOR
text: And I will give a taste thereof forthwith

speaker: NESTOR
text: To Agamemnon. Go we to him straight.

speaker: NESTOR
text: Two curs shall tame each other; pride alone

speaker: NESTOR
text: Must tar the mastiffs on, as ’twere a bone.
